Slightly higher securityRisk score (0.1), but justified because it is a generic factory, not executing external code or handling untrusted input.  \n- **Scores:** malware: 0, obfuscated: 0, securityRisk: 0.1, confidence: 1.  \n- **Assessment:** Slightly elevated securityRisk score is acceptable given the nature of the code; no malicious behavior.\n\n**Report 4:**  \n- **Findings:** Same as above, standard autogenerated factory, no suspicious elements.  \n- **Scores:** malware: 0, obfuscated: 0, securityRisk: 0, confidence: 1.  \n- **Assessment:** Appropriate and consistent.\n\n**Report 5:**  \n- **Findings:** Also a standard autogenerated contract wrapper, with mention of a source map (common in debug builds). No malicious activity or obfuscation.  \n- **Scores:** malware: 0, obfuscated: 0, securityRisk: 0, confidence: 0.95.  \n- **Assessment:** The slight reduction in confidence is reasonable due to the presence of a source map, which is typical but can sometimes be exploited if not handled properly. Still, no signs of malicious intent.\n\n---\n\n### Cross-Analysis & Final Judgment:\n\n- **Presence of Malicious Code:**  \n  All reports agree that the code is a standard autogenerated factory with no malicious behavior. The malware score is consistently 0, which is justified.\n\n- **Obfuscation:**  \n  The code is straightforward, with no obfuscation patterns. Scores are 0 across reports.\n\n- **Security Risks:**  \n  The only minor concern is the slightly elevated securityRisk score in Report 3 (0.1), justified by the fact that the code is a simple factory, not executing untrusted code or handling external data directly. The other reports assign 0, which is appropriate.\n\n- **Logic & Reasoning Flaws:**  \n  No logical flaws or mistakes are evident in the reports. They all correctly identify the code as a benign, autogenerated contract wrapper.\n\n- **Scores Consistency:**  \n  The scores are consistent with the analysis.
